segment-text: 7-14-19 Based on the novel "8-Bit Christmas" by Kevin Jakubowski
EXT. CHICAGO - ESTABLISHING - MORNING - DAY
A cold but sunny morning in Chicago. Snow glistens on trees. A jingle bell rings in the distance. Christmas is in the air.
EXT. MICHIGAN AVENUE - MORNING - DAY
COMMUTERS file down sidewalks and bridges. A sea of boots and coats. One man stands out. He is JAKE DOYLE (40, stressed, but can laugh about it). He talks on his iPhone as he walks.
...What do you mean you didn't send it yet? We need the shipment, Ed. We needed it yesterday.
Dad look! That's the tablet I want!
Jake's daughter ANNIE DOYLE (8, inquisitive) points to a storefront DISPLAY of the latest TABLET. Annie sniffles with a cold but her eyes are curious, alert.
Can we go inside? Can we go look? Jake holds up a finger, "just a second", stays on the phone.
I don't care if it's almost Christmas. Get us the-- whoa!
Jake steps on a patch of ice and almost loses his balance. He shoots a look at a nearby SALVATION ARMY SANTA CLAUS (60s).
Still?! The ice?!
I told 'em to salt it.
Get us the shipment Ed. Because I just yelled at Santa. Yeah. You're making me the guy who yells at Santa. My daughter is very upset.
Jake holds the phone down to Annie, she's all smiles.
I'm very upset!
Jake chuckles, takes the phone back.
See? Get us the shipment!
Jake takes Annie's hand as they hustle across the street.
INT. ELEVATOR - SKYSCRAPER - A LITTLE LATER - MORNING - DAY
Jake and Annie ride an elevator up to Jake's office. Annie blows her nose as she watches Jake scroll through emails.
Are you stressed Dad? Are you
tired? Is this elevator safe? Jake's phone buzzes. It's his WIFE calling.
Hold on, it's Mom. (on phone) Hey. Yeah, it's strep. We got the antibiotic. When can you come get her? Me? Honey, I can't watch her all day. Because I just can't.
Annie sighs, she's used to this. Jake doesn't notice her reaction. He just listens to his wife, softening a little.
Okay. No, you're right. I can watch
her. I'm not too busy. The doors open. Jake exits.
I got this.
A beat. Annie is still in the elevator. The doors start to close. Jake runs back and pulls her out. ADULT JAKE Totally got it.
INT. JAKE'S OFFICE - MAIN FLOOR - MOMENTS LATER - DAY
Jake leads Annie through his office. Employees bustle about.
Do all these people work here? What
time does work start? Are you late? A coworker, BETH (30s) passes by.
Pendrock keeps calling from New York, Jake.
I know, I know.
INT. JAKE'S CORNER OFFICE - MOMENTS LATER - DAY
Jake walks Annie into his spacious corner office. BLUEPRINTS and PICTURES adorn the walls. Annie looks out the window.
Is that a park down there? Is that
a jungle gym? Is this office ecofriendly?
Okay, no more questions. You're sick. You need to take a nap.
That's boring.
Jake gently leads Annie to a couch, takes off her coat.
If I had my own tablet I wouldn't be bored.
Enough about the tablet, sweetie, okay? C'mere.
Jake sets a pillow down and Annie reluctantly lies down.
There we go. Now, I'm gonna go over there and send about three hundred emails and you're gonna lie here and get some rest. Cool? Cool.
Jake heads to his desk, gets right back to work.
Are you gonna work over Christmas again too?
(not paying attention) Uh-huh, sounds good.
Annie sighs, disappointed. Bored, she starts to look around the room. Something in the corner sparks her interest. Jake looks up to see Annie now standing before his OLD TV.
Hey, you're supposed to be resting.
What's that?
Annie points to an OLD NINTENDO under the TV. ADULT JAKE A Nintendo.
A video game from when I was little.
Cool. Can we play?
Annie, I have work to do-ANNIE Jake sighs. He needs a play here. Beth walks by his door.
What's up?
ADULT JAKE
Jake heads to the door, out of ear shot from Annie.
Can you push the Pendrock call for a bit. I gotta get her down for a
nap somehow.
No problem.
Jake heads back to Annie who is now feebly trying to get the Nintendo to work, pressing buttons, etc. Jake picks up a GAME from a KANGAROOS SHOE BOX full of games on the coffee table.
Here. First you have to do this.
Jake blows on the game as is customary.
I don't know, you just do.
Jake hands the game to Annie, she blows on it happily.
I thought you said Grandma and Grandpa didn't let you have video games when you were little.
They didn't.
Then how did you get it?
Tell you what? If I tell you the story of how I got my Nintendo will you take a nap? A long one? Uh-huh.

You have to promise though. Because this is probably the most amazing, dangerous, awesome story of all time and you'll probably get so excited you'll never want to sleep ever again so you have to promise. Promise.
ANNIE
Jake puts the game in the console, has a seat next to Annie.
Okay. The year was 1987. Or was it '88? (thinking to himself) '85 Bears. Super Bowl in '86...
Does it really matter Dad?
You wanna hear this or not?
Annie does. Jake hands her a controller, she smiles. EXCITE BIKE starts to play on TV. Jake softens a little, settles in.
The year was 1988. Let's call it
The sounds of an 8-Bit motor-cross bike rev as we...
EXT. SUBURBAN CHICAGO STREET - 1988 - MORNING - DAY

The Excite Bike sound effects now match a BOY on his bike. This is YOUNG JAKE DOYLE (10). He pedals down the middle of the street as fast as his legs can carry him. He wears a ski jacket, Walter Payton Kangaroo shoes and a Bears knit cap.
In the winter, if the streets were plowed, I could make it to Timmy Kleen's house in just under eight minutes.
On a bike? How fast were you going?
Super fast.
Did you wear a helmet?
Uh, yeah. We always wore helmets in the 80s. We were super safe.
A HELMET suddenly appears on Jake's head.
What color was it?
Just let me tell the story, Annie.
WHOOSH! Jake rounds a corner and flies down another street. The Excite Bike sound effects match his increase in speed. Suburban middle class houses pass him by.
INT. SUBURBAN HOUSE - FAMILY ROOM - CONTINUOUS
MIKEY TROTTER (10) watches INSPECTOR GADGET, eating cereal. ADULT JAKE (V.O.)
conclusion that Inspector Gadget
On TV DR. CLAW yells his famous line as he makes his escape.
I'll get you next time Gadget!
(to TV, disappointed) You kiddin' me?
Trotter looks up through the window and notices Jake whizzing by. He quickly drops his cereal and rushes out of the room.
EXT. STREET - MORNING - CONTINUOUS

Jake pedals on, determined. He approaches a snowblower and rides through its snow shower. Nothing will get in his way. ADULT JAKE (V.O.) The rule at Kleen's was simple. The first ten kids to get through his door got to play. And the first one on his couch got first game, after
Jake pulls even with a PAPER BOY (10) on his bike delivering papers. The two lock eyes. Jake accelerates past him. The Paper Boy reluctantly picks up his pace.
EXT. TIMMY KLEEN'S HOUSE - MOMENTS LATER - DAY

Jake pedals up to TIMMY KLEEN'S HOUSE. It's by far the biggest and nicest one on the block. In one continuous motion Jake hops off his bike and ditches it in the yard. Momentum carrying him, Jake runs up the front steps and rings the bell repeatedly. He takes his helmet off, noticing it for the first time and chucks it over his shoulder. A sleepy TIFFANY KLEEN (16) answers the door. She struggles to hold a yipping little dog LACY DOG from getting out.
Lacy Dog! NO! You're too early, I
don't even think he's up-Jake runs past her right into the house.
--yet. Hey!
INT. TIMMY KLEEN'S BASEMENT - CONTINUOUS

Jake comes running down the basement steps into a nice rec room. He flicks on a light, heroically illuminating a NINTENDO ENTERTAINMENT SYSTEM sitting under a HUGE TV on the far side of the room. Jake stands awestruck. ADULT JAKE (V.O.) And there she was, glistening in all her gray plastic glory. A maze of rubber wiring and electronic
As Jake smiles we see a RETAINER in his mouth.
There wasn't a pair of blistered kid thumbs in suburbia that didn't
feel an instinctive tingle when the word Nintendo was mentioned.
Forget something? (O.S.)

Jake turns around to see TIMMY KLEEN (10) at the bottom of the stairs. He wears a KARATE ROBE over silk PJ's and stirs chocolate milk in a coffee cup. Remember the rich kid in the neighborhood with all the cool stuff? This is him.
It was an early lesson in God's cruelty giving the only Nintendo in
town to rich kid Timmy Kleen.
Your shoes, butthead.
Oh, sorry, sorry about that.
Kleen heads upstairs. Jake follows, tiptoeing in his shoes.
I'd prayed several times to be made
part of his family.
INT/EXT. TIMMY KLEEN'S HOUSE - MOMENTS LATER - FOYER

Jake sets his shoes by the door. Timmy stirs his chocolate milk and gazes out the window. TWO DOZEN KIDS are now jostling for position outside in the snow, hoping to get in. ADULT JAKE (V.O.) Outside, half of H.C Storm Elementary wrestled in the snow.
Jake looks at a particular group of KIDS, his friends. FREEZE ON: MIKEY TROTTER. On screen, a wood border frames him up like an '87 TOPPS BASEBALL CARD, name and logo included. ADULT JAKE (V.O.)
How much you guys think a flame thrower costs?
Flame throwers are dangerous.
BASEBALL CARD FREEZE ON: EVAN OLSEN (10) looking nervous.
Evan Olsen. Nervous. Allergic to bees. Probably has to go home in ten minutes.
I prolly gotta go home soon.
More Nintendo for us.
BASEBALL CARD FREEZE ON: TOMMY GRUSECKI (10, smart) as he studies a BECKETT BASEBALL CARD MAGAZINE. ADULT JAKE (V.O.)
genius. Future multi-millionaire. UN FREEZE.
Hey look at that. My Canseco rookie's up two bucks.
The Canseco's mine. Is not.
Is too! It was in my pack!
BASEBALL CARD FREEZE ON: RYAN GRUSECKI pushing his brother. ADULT JAKE (V.O.)
brother. At least I think that's Ryan. It was always hard to tell them apart.
UN FREEZE: Ryan and Tommy go at it.
Yep. That was my crew. These were the guys who'd make it all happen.
(with a lisp) Hey guys! What's up?!
Oh yeah, and this kid.
JOSH FARMER (10, serious lisp) runs into frame.
Just got off the phone with Whitney Houston, she says hi.
BASEBALL CARD FREEZE ON: Josh's smiling face.
Josh Farmer. Pathological liar. Not really our friend, but always around.
I tell you I saw Bigfoot in the bushes outside my Dad's apartment last night. Intense.
The same apartment you saw Randy the Macho Man Savage at?
That's the one.
And he told you wrestling was real?
You guys have good memories.
You never saw Macho Man, Farmer.
Did too. He was eating a hot dog.
Macho Man eats Slim Jims.
I know, I thought it was weird too. BOY (O.S.) Hey! He's coming out!

A hush falls over the crowd. Everyone turns to the porch. The door slowly opens and out steps Timmy. He stands high above the crowd on the top step. Lacey Dog nips at his heels. ADULT JAKE (V.O.) Kleen surveyed the crowd like an annoyed Roman emperor. He checked his Swatch watch. Both of them. Kleen checks his wrist, revealing two Swatch watches.
Eight AM. Game time.
Anyone for a little Nintendo?
The crowd ROARS! Everyone rushes toward the door. Fresh to the scene, the Paper Boy from earlier pedals through the crowd chucking papers indiscriminately. It's pandemonium.
INT. KLEEN'S BASEMENT - MOMENTS LATER
Today's TEN LUCKY KIDS file downstairs, out of breath.
Boots off, boots off. Don't touch
the wall it's French.
Wait, my brother's still outside.
You know the rules. First ten, that's it.
But it's Ryan.
(mimicking)
"But it's Ryan". Ryan Grusecki bangs on the adjacent basement well window.
Kleen just closes the curtain shut.
You guys want some Pop Tarts or something?
Yeah that'd be great. Too bad.
Kleen chuckles to himself as the boys seat themselves numerically on the couch. Kleen turns on DUCK HUNT, grabs the GUN and holds it directly on the TV. He smiles devilishly.
Winner stays.
Kleen blasts away, he'll be playing forever. Jake scowls.
As I sat there and watched Timmy Kleen blast away to his evil heart's content...
Die duck! Die! Ha ha ha!
One thing became clear: I needed to get my own Nintendo. Fast.
INT. DOYLE HOUSE - KITCHEN - NIGHT
An old, two story, Victorian style farm house. Jake sits at the kitchen table lost in thought, spinning his retainer.
For two months I'd been secretly laying the ground work for my
Nintendo Christmas sales pitch.
INT. DOYLE HOUSE - KITCHEN - DAY - QUICK POP - FLASHBACK
Jake proudly hangs a crude DRAWING on the refrigerator of a family playing Nintendo together.
Carefully crafting pictures...
INT. DOYLE MINI VAN - DAY - QUICK POP - FLASHBACK
Jake sits in the back seat, singing the theme to Super Mario Brothers, loudly. "Do-do-do-do-do-do-do..."
Quietly singing theme songs. FRONT SEAT VOICE (O.S.)
INT. DOYLE HOUSE - KITCHEN - DAY - QUICK POP - FLASHBACK
Jake sits at the kitchen table eating NINTENDO CEREAL.
And making ever so subtle suggestions.
Oh my God! This is the best cereal I've ever had in my life!
INT. DOYLE HOUSE - KITCHEN - NIGHT - CONTINUOUS
Jake sits up in his chair, centers himself.
But tonight was the night I'd finally put it all into action.
Jake's mom PATTY DOYLE (40, even keeled) cooks chili on the stove. She wears a leotard and leg warmers.
You know what's really important mom? Hand-eye coordination.
(didn't hear him) What's that dear?
Hand-eye coordination. Totally. You need it to get into college.
Is that right.
CRASH! Something falls in the other room.
God bless it!
Well I'm sure your dad will want to teach it to you then.
JOHN DOYLE (40s, gruff) storms into the kitchen through a
plastic sheet hanging over the door to the dining room. He wears a tool belt and is slightly dusted in plaster dust.
Has anyone seen my bandsaw?
I think it's in the shower.
That's the table saw. I need the bandsaw. Jake get in here.
John grabs a package of nails off the counter and goes back through the sheet. Jake reluctantly follows.
INT. DOYLE HOUSE - DINING ROOM - MOMENTS LATER
Jake awkwardly holds a board as John hammers nails into it.
It was a well known up and down
Linwood Avenue that in 1978 my
Dinner's ready! You almost finished out there?
John gives a quick glance around. The room, like much of the house, is totally gutted.
You know what I mean.
He was warmly referred to in our house as the dyslexic Bob Vila.
John examines his work. Not satisfied.
God bless it.
And no matter what, if I was ever going to get a Nintendo, I'd have to go through him.
INT. DOYLE HOUSE - KITCHEN - A LITTLE LATER
Jake sits at the table eating dinner with his parents and his little sister LIZZY DOYLE (6, super smart, cute, conniving).
This was going to take some serious buttering up.
Drywall looks really good in there Dad. Totally.
Stop saying totally. It makes you sound like a moron. And why's your retainer out of your mouth?
We're eating.
Do you know how expensive that thing is? Put it back in.
Jake looks at his mom for some help.
John you really can't eat with it in. It doesn't work that way.
Well it's terribly designed then.
If I had a retainer I'd wear it all the time. No matter what.
I know you would Lizzy. Good girl.
Lizzy smiles devilishly at Jake. Jake scowls.
You haven't seen my bandsaw have you?
It's in the backyard. Right next to all the dog poop that Jake didn't pick up.
You didn't pick that up?! Cry in a bucket, Jake. What have you been doing all day?
Well Dad, I've been playing an amazing new system called Nintendo! You seem ready to hear about it, let me regale you with its wonders!
Uh, I was in Timmy Kleen's basement.
You can't play outside? It's cold.
It's not even below zero out. What the heck were you doing inside all day?
Probably playing the Nintenda.
Nintendo. Tenda?
PATTY DOYLE
Tendo. The Nintendo Entertainment System. It's this totally-- really educational piece of technology, Dad. I think you'd like it.
Well I ran into Mrs. Trotter at the Jewel today. Apparently Nintendo's been doing all kinds of strange things to children in Japan.
Like making them smarter? Faster? Better at sports and Karate?
They get so involved that they forget about everything else. School, friends. One little boy supposedly had a seizure. A seizure?
Plus they're very expensive.
Video games make you fat Jake. That is a fact.
Cabbage Patch Kids don't make you fat. And you can take them outside. They're a great Christmas present.
Lizzy smiles at Jake. She's good and she knows it.
No Nintendo in this house. I'll tell you that right now.
I second that.
Nintendo no-friendo.
Now what did you want to talk to us about honey? Something important you said?
Jake just sighs and stares at his plate, crushed.
Never mind.
MIKEY TROTTER (PRE-LAP) Jake. Jake...
EXT. HC STORM ELEMENTARY SCHOOL - PLAYGROUND - DAY

Jake sits in a daze slumped against the bottom of a MASSIVE SNOW MOUND on the playground. Trotter and Olsen crouch on either side of him like a couple of scared infantry men. In the distance, we hear the sounds of screaming. Welcome to fifth grade. Jake!
Jake snaps out of it and looks up.
You gotta focus man.
His parents told him he couldn't get a Nintendo last night, go easy.
Jeez. You sure you're up for this? Yeah...

The guys peer up toward the top of the mound. Standing there, dominating the game "King of the Mountain" is DAN DELUND (age unknown). He wears steel toed work boots and a Motley Crue tshirt under his ratty bomber jacket. His rat tail is amazing.
It was never quite certain what
grade Dan "King of the Mountain"
Franklin cigarette counter.
rush up to him he just tosses them off like rag dolls.
Woooo! You like that?!
And every day before school we would gather, for reasons unknown, to get pummeled by him in the game
"King of the Mountain." Tommy Grusecki and Ryan Grusecki come tumbling down the mound face first and land on either side of the group.
I think I saw hair under his pits.
Trotter keeps his eyes on Delund, focused.
Okay, his back is turned. This is it. You ready Jake? Right now?
Olsen? Goonies never say die, man.
Goonies never met Dan Delund.
Let's DO IIIIIIIT!

Trotter charges up the hill, yelling. Jake sighs, then reluctantly follows after him. Olsen sits there for a beat, then nervously charges up the hill too. Athletic and confident, Trotter sprints toward Delund.
Delund spins around, his eyes widening. In one powerful motion he just grabs Trotter and tosses him over the side. Noooo!
Jake and Olsen see this happening but it's too late to retreat. Delund clothes-lines both of them at the same time with two outstretched arms. They hit the ground with a THUD.
My mountain! Mine!
Olsen somehow rolls himself off the hill. Jake tries to crawl away but Delund grabs him and starts white washing his face.
Ha! Ha! Ha!
Quit it! Quit it! Quit it!
Delund tosses Jake over the side, raises his arms in victory. Mine!
Jake comes tumbling down and lands next to Olsen, Trotter and the Gruseckis who all lie flat on their backs, dazed.
I hate that guy.
Just think about the Power Glove, man.
What Power Glove?
You didn't tell him?
I thought you told him. The Power Glove! Kleen got one last night!
Evan pulls out a NINTENDO POWER MAGAZINE from his back pocket, points to a POWER GLOVE ad. Jake's in awe. No way...
He's already practicing, look.
Jake and Olsen look to the other side of the playground where Kleen practices martial arts "Power Glove" moves with his naked right hand, chopping and punching at imaginary enemies.
We're all going over to his house after school to play it.
He's gonna let us in?
We gotta bring him gifts, but yeah.
Who else knows about it?
Just us. Well, and Farmer.
Farmer knows? Great.
EXT. TIMMY KLEEN'S HOUSE - LATER THAT DAY - DAY
Timmy Kleen's front lawn is SWARMING with KIDS, at least twice as many as Saturday. The atmosphere is like a rock concert. The crowd chants in unison. CROWD POW-ER GLOVE! POW-ER GLOVE! POW-ER GLOVE!
I didn't say anything to anybody. Swear to God.
The Gruseckis, Jake, Trotter and Olsen just stare at Farmer.
Okay, maybe Meg Platt. And Steve Dybsky. And my second period gym class and some lunch ladies but that's it.
Come on Farmer! We're never gonna get in now.
Don't worry, I got a Power Glove too, you guys can play it whenever.
No you don't Farmer.
No I do not.
I don't think anybody in Chicago has one yet. I don't even know how the thing works.
Nobody knows how it works, man.
Oh, it has a suction cup that connects directly to your brain. Yeah. My uncle was on the design team. (then) At NASA. (then, off their looks) I hate you guys.
Suddenly the front door starts to unlock. Everybody turns.
He's coming out! Tiffany Kleen walks out with a BOOMBOX, rolling her eyes.
This is so dumb.
A voice yell-whispers behind her through the mail slot.
Just do it.

Tiffany reluctantly presses play. The 80s anthem "HOLDING OUT FOR A HERO" by Bonnie Tyler plays. Tiffany opens the door and Timmy struts out onto the porch. He wears sunglasses and his Karate robe. His right hand is stuffed into his robe pocket. The crowd is silent. Not sure what to make of this. But then, Kleen shoots his right hand up in the air to the beat. Sure enough he's wearing the POWER GLOVE. The crowd goes bananas! HYSTERICAL KID Yeah Kleen! YEEEEEAAAAAH! Jake just stares at the glove in awe.
It was the most beautiful piece of machinery I'd ever laid eyes on. The thing made Luke Skywalker's
robot hand look like a Tinker Toy. Kleen silences the crowd with one swipe of his hand.
Silence children of Batavia!
I'm not kidding, that's seriously how this kid talked.
What have you brought me?!
Everyone holds up toys and money. Jake holds up a SAUSAGE.
It was the best I could find.
You're not getting in dude.
The crowd surges. Kleen starts pointing to individual kids, who hold up and shout out their offerings.
Five bucks from my first communion!
A bunch a Micro Machines! Two.
I can get you a Ryne Sandberg rookie card by tomorrow--! No.
Over here! Timmy! It's gourmet! It's a gourmet sausage!
Kleen's not interested. Jake's not going to make the cut. Lacy dog barks like a maniac. Jake watches her run to the back of the house. Suddenly he gets an idea.
EXT. KLEEN'S HOUSE - BACK OF THE HOUSE - CONTINUOUS - DAY

Jake runs to the back of the house to see Lacy Dog run through a TINY DOG DOOR. ADULT JAKE (V.O.) Desperate times called for desperate measures. There was no way I was missing out on that Power Glove. Jake runs to the doggie door. As he pokes his head in Lacy Dog starts barking and nipping at his face.
Ah! Shh! Lacy Dog, no!
Lacy Dog won't shut up. Jake suddenly remembers the sausage. He tosses it inside. The barking stops. Jake dives through.
INT. TIMMY KLEEN'S HOUSE - FOYER - MOMENTS LATER
Jake runs through the house and casually files in with the group now walking down into the basement.
How did you--?
Jake just smiles as he removes his shoes.
INT. TIMMY KLEEN'S HOUSE - BASEMENT - MOMENTS LATER
Ten lucky kids and Jake sit facing Kleen who stands dramatically before them. He raises his gloved fist.
Gentlemen. Behold! The power of the Power Glove!
Kleen presses play on the dual deck boom box.
Timmy's audio journal. Well, I peed
on myself a little bit today-Kleen shuts off the tape. He pressed the wrong tape deck.
No one heard that!
Everyone stifles their laughter.
Behold! The power of the Power Glove!
Kleen presses play on the other tape deck and "DANGER ZONE" by Kenny Loggins plays. Way too into it, Kleen starts playing DOUBLE DRAGON, a violent fighting game. Everyone watches, excited.
Oh man this was it! The Power Glove at last! Each one of us in that basement knew we were about to witness history.
Kleen starts throwing punches.
We just didn't know what kind yet.
I said hi-ya! Kleen has trouble controlling his character on screen.
You gotta kick him Kleen.
I'm trying! Hi-ya! Hi-ya!
Kleen's character just stands there, getting pummeled.
You're getting killed man.
Do what I say! Do what I say!
Kleen jiggles the cord connected to the glove. He tries a few different buttons, nothing works.
What's wrong with this thing?!
And that's when we realized. In the span of about fifteen seconds. A horror that has haunted our generation ever since.
This thing sucks.
The Power Glove sucked.
It doesn't work at all!
It didn't work at all. NOOOOOOOO!
Maybe it was the glove. Maybe it was the song. Maybe it was because he was just such a magnificent spaz. But Timmy Kleen lost his dang mind that day.
Kleen loses it. He starts flailing about. Pounding the carpet. Kicking and screaming. Lacy Dog barks like a maniac.
Stupid glove! Stupid game! Why are you so stupid?!
The boys watch in amusement as Kleen starts to karate chop and kick anything he can find.
You tell 'em Kleen!
Yeah, show us your moves Kleen!
The taunting probably didn't help either.
Go Kleen go! Tae Kwan Do! EVERYONE Go Kleen go! Tae Kwon Do! Go Kleen go! Tae Kwon Do!
The couch is in hysterics, chanting. Kleen starts doing Karate moves in a blind rage. Lacy Dog barks like crazy.
ADULT JAKE (V.O.)
Kleen suddenly locks his eyes on a target across the room and we go into SLOW MOTION...
RRRRAAAAHHHHHH!
Kleen charges the TV. He takes flight and jump kicks the screen. It rocks back and forth on impact. NOOOOO!
EVERYONE

We now notice Lacy Dog sitting directly under it, still barking. The TV lurches forward. The kids all watch it fall to the ground. The barking stops. ADULT JAKE (V.O.) Broken stained glass window. Swallowed marble. Show Biz Pizza
lying under the weight a smashed
ANNIE (V.O.) INT. JAKE'S OFFICE - LATER - PRESENT DAY - DAY
Back to present day. Annie's face fills the frame, upset.
The dog dies?! There's a dead dog in this story?!
It was the eighties, what can I tell you. Stuff got real.
We notice that Annie and Jake have been playing Super Mario Brothers. The game has been paused on the TV.
Are you making all of this up? No way.
ADULT JAKE
I don't believe you. I'm Googling Nintendo.
Be my guest. Jake?
BETH (O.S.)
Beth knocks on Jake's door. Jake gets up to answer it. Annie nibbles on a cookie as she heads toward Jake's computer.
And no more cookies. This story's backfiring on me here, you're supposed to be asleep by now.
Jake approaches Beth at the door. What's up? ADULT JAKE
I pushed it an hour, but they want an answer.
Beth hands Jake a large file, gives him a hopeful look.
It's a big contract, Jake.
I know, I know.
You want another Christmas cookie Annie?
Yes please!
She does not. Thank you Beth.
Jake turns to Annie who is now on his computer.
So Power Glove didn't come out til 1989. And Nintendo came out in 1985 not 1988. Your dates are all wrong.
Well I guess I should stop telling the story then.
No! I mean, you can still tell it if you want.
And then you'll take a nap?
Uh-huh. What happened next? In the basement? What did you do?
Jake looks at his file for a second. Then at Annie.
Well, we did what every other rational, thoughtful, intelligent kid would've done in our position.
You ran away? So fast.
ADULT JAKE EXT. TIMMY KLEEN'S HOUSE - MOMENTS LATER - DAY

WHOOSH! Kleen's front door flies open and eleven screaming kids come pouring out. Some try to put their boots on as they run, others just sprint away in their stocking feet. Evan Olsen and a few other stragglers sit outside.
What happened?! What happened?!
Get outta here!
Jake holds his shoes in his hands and hops on the back of Trotter's bike as Trotter pedals away.
It was the endo of Nintendo as we knew it. So many thoughts raced through my head. Would I be arrested? Would I go to jail? Or worse, would my parents find out?
INT. DOYLE HOUSE - KITCHEN - NIGHT
Jake sits at the table, nervously picking at his dinner.
So. What happened today? What did you do after school, Jake?
Nothing to do with a certain dog I bet.
Oh god. This was it. This was the end.
